mechanism: Participants evaluate behavioral scenarios and apply the concept of intentionality. When agents bring about effects they foresaw but did not aim for, judgments of intentionality vary based on moral valence of the outcome. Moral considerations influence semantic classification through dual-method verification: explicit intentionality judgments correlate with reason-explanation acceptance (sentence acceptability test).

Randomized field experiment: emails with names signaling racial/ethnic identity are sent to government bureaucrats; response rates and tone (named salutation) are measured across treatment groups; statistical models compare responsiveness and tone across races while controlling for community demographics. Multiple logistic regression tests for main effects and interactions.

Observers estimate weight of body images and discriminate between pairs differing in body mass. Contraction bias predicts systematic under/over-estimation relative to population mean; Weber's law predicts just-noticeable-difference scales linearly with stimulus magnitude. Regression tests slope parameters; psychometric functions fitted via probit analysis.
distribution: visual estimation treated as noisy measurement; normal distribution assumed for regression

mechanism: Student trait (theory of intelligence) measured at baseline; trajectories of math grades tracked over junior high school transition via hierarchical linear modeling. Mediational analysis tests whether motivational constructs (learning goals, effort beliefs, attributions, strategies) explain the theory-achievement relation; structural equation modeling tests process model.
distribution: grades treated as continuous; growth assumed linear within segments

mechanism: Randomized intervention assigns students to learn malleable-intelligence lesson or control content; grades tracked pre/post-intervention using hierarchical linear modeling with knot-point (change-point) to detect discontinuity. Teacher reports of behavior coded for positive motivational change. Process mediated by learning goals, effort beliefs, and strategies.
distribution: grades continuous; growth piecewise-linear around intervention

mechanism: Visual search task: participants detect target item in display of varying size; response time slope indicates parallel vs. serial search. Regression of RT on display size per observer; ANOVA on slope/intercept parameters. Tests whether three-dimensional scene-based orientation permits preattentive feature detection comparable to image-based orientation.
distribution: reaction times modeled via regression; normally distributed assumed
